segments.title: Yak-38 as the overlooked Cold War V/STOL fighter
segments.summary: The opening positions the Yak-38 as the other Cold War V/STOL fighter, often overshadowed by the Harrier but not simply a failure.
segments.body: The value is the comparison frame: the transcript notes that the F-35B hover arrangement is closer to Yak-38 engineering than to Harrier, while still calling the Forger a first-generation carrier fighter.

evidence.note: Opening compares Yak-38 with Harrier and F-35B while setting up its first-generation V/STOL role.

segments.title: Yak-38 automatic flight-control system
segments.summary: The aircraft needed an automatic flight-control system to handle its V/STOL control demands.
segments.body: The discussion isolates the engineering reason the Yak-38 was more than a simple Harrier comparison.

evidence.note: The aircraft needed an automatic flight-control system to handle its V/STOL control demands.

segments.title: Yak-38 was limited but not simply bad for its time
segments.summary: The transcript says the aircraft was not a bad aircraft for its time, but a surprise in what it achieved.
segments.body: The discussion captures the episode corrective against easy dismissal of the Forger.

evidence.note: The transcript says the aircraft was not a bad aircraft for its time, but a surprise in what it achieved.

segments.title: What Yak-38 did for Kiev
segments.summary: The episode asks what the aircraft was actually doing for the Kiev class.
segments.body: The discussion connects aircraft limitations to the ship mission and Soviet naval aviation requirements.

evidence.note: The episode asks what the aircraft was actually doing for the Kiev class.

segments.title: Kiev sortie record as capability evidence
segments.summary: The later section cites Yak-38 sorties from Kiev as evidence that the capability had operational substance.
segments.body: This gives the page a concrete operations window instead of only design comparison.

evidence.note: The later section cites Yak-38 sorties from Kiev as evidence that the capability had operational substance.

segments.title: Yak-38 legacy and better Kiev counterfactual
segments.summary: The closing asks what the Yak-38 legacy would look like if the aircraft and Kiev class had been better matched.
segments.body: The discussion captures the counterfactual and legacy evaluation near the end.

evidence.note: The closing asks what the Yak-38 legacy would look like if the aircraft and Kiev class had been better matched.
